headline: Tesla Stock Is Falling. It Isn’t the Delivery Numbers.
summary: Tesla  stock fell in premarket trading Thursday, ahead of its release of first-quarter delivery figures, as investors grew nervous about renewed conflict in the Middle East.  Benchmark international crude oil prices were up 8% in early trading on Thursday, above $109 per barrel.  Coming into Thursday trading, the S&P 500 was up 3.2% for the week, leaving it down 4.4% since the fighting in Iran began.

headline: Tesla's deliveries miss estimates as tax credit expiry weighs
summary:
STORY: Tesla missed Wall Street expectations for first-quarter deliveries.

The auto giant saw demand hit by the expiry of U.S. tax credits on the purchase of electric vehicles.

It delivered just over 358,000 vehicles in the January-March period - below market expectations of close to 369,000.

That was down 14.4% from the fourth quarter, but it was up 6.3% from a year earlier.

Thursday's update marked the first time Tesla had posted two consecutive years of falling deliveries.

Analysts have cut their forecasts for deliveries this year, and some warned of a third straight annual drop.

Tesla lost its title as the world's largest EV maker last year to China's BYD.

There were some green shoots in Europe, a region that weighed on Tesla's global figures last year.

The company has shown signs of recovery there and gained share in key markets like France in the first quarter.

Tesla's China-made EV sales also rose for a second consecutive quarter.

They were up just over 23% for the January to March period - well up from a near 2% rise in the fourth quarter.

But the expiry of a $7,500 federal tax credit at the end of September was a blow to U.S. EV demand.

Analysts expect the credit's removal to hurt EV sales this year.

They further said Tesla also faces growing competition in the region.

Shares were down around 4% after the update.

headline: Tesla sales rise after brutal year of Musk boycotts but still fall short of expectations
summary: Sales of Tesla vehicles rose in the past three months in a possible sign recovery is afoot after a brutal year of boycotts over Elon Musk's politics but still fell short of expectations.  The positive figure follows a year of plunging sales due to an aging lineup and boycotts over Musk's right-wing political stands.  The sales in the three months through March were 6% lower than the 381,000 that financial analysts had expected, according to a survey by researcher FactSet.

content.title: Tesla sales rise after brutal year of Musk boycotts but still fall short of expectations

The sales in the three months through March were 6% lower than the 381,000 that financial analysts had expected, according to a survey by researcher FactSet.

headline: Blue Owl limits fund withdrawals, mortgage rates rise for fifth week
summary: Yahoo Finance Senior Producer John Hyland tracks today's top moving stocks and biggest market stories in this Market Minute.US stocks (^DJI, ^GSPC, ^IXIC) begin the second quarter under pressure as concerns around private credit and higher mortgage rates weigh on sentiment.Blue Owl Capital (OWL, OBDC) stock falls after the firm said it will limit redemptions from two of its largest private credit funds.Tesla (TSLA) stock drops after its first quarter deliveries miss Wall Street estimates.US mortgage rates rise for the fifth straight week.

content.title: Blue Owl limits fund withdrawals, mortgage rates rise for fifth week
content.description:
Yahoo Finance Senior Producer John Hyland tracks today's top moving stocks and biggest market stories in this Market Minute.

US stocks (^DJI, ^GSPC, ^IXIC) begin the second quarter under pressure as concerns around private credit and higher mortgage rates weigh on sentiment.

Blue Owl Capital (OWL, OBDC) stock falls after the firm said it will limit redemptions from two of its largest private credit funds.

Tesla (TSLA) stock drops after its first quarter deliveries miss Wall Street estimates.

US mortgage rates rise for the fifth straight week.
content.summary: Yahoo Finance Senior Producer John Hyland tracks today's top moving stocks and biggest market stories in this Market Minute.US stocks (^DJI, ^GSPC, ^IXIC) begin the second quarter under pressure as concerns around private credit and higher mortgage rates weigh on sentiment.Blue Owl Capital (OWL, OBDC) stock falls after the firm said it will limit redemptions from two of its largest private credit funds.Tesla (TSLA) stock drops after its first quarter deliveries miss Wall Street estimates.US mortgage rates rise for the fifth straight week.

headline: Tesla Sales Rebound From Last Year’s Slump
summary: After a year marked by sluggish electric-vehicle sales, Tesla’s final tally for the first quarter was 358,023 EVs delivered globally, missing analysts’ estimates of 368,900 deliveries, according to a consensus of 19 investment firms’ estimates published by Visible Alpha.  Despite the growth from the year-ago quarter, it was Tesla’s second worst quarter of sales since 2022.  The growth in deliveries comes as Tesla pivots its business away from vehicle sales to focus on robotaxis and humanoid robots, neither of which is currently for sale.

content.title: Tesla Sales Rebound From Last Year’s Slump
content.summary: After a year marked by sluggish electric-vehicle sales, Tesla’s final tally for the first quarter was 358,023 EVs delivered globally, missing analysts’ estimates of 368,900 deliveries, according to a consensus of 19 investment firms’ estimates published by Visible Alpha.  Despite the growth from the year-ago quarter, it was Tesla’s second worst quarter of sales since 2022.  The growth in deliveries comes as Tesla pivots its business away from vehicle sales to focus on robotaxis and humanoid robots, neither of which is currently for sale.
